title: Indulgent English Speck Scones Clotted Cream Pastries
description: A tangy, snappy, one-pot British dish featuring speck, en-croute, gluten-free-friendly, forming scones with clotted cream.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large mixing bowl, combine sugar, almond flour, and salt. Whisk until well combined.
Add chilled butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs and heavy cream. Add vanilla extract and whisk until smooth.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and gently knead it a few times until it comes together.
Roll the dough out to about 1 inch (2.5 cm) thickness. Use a biscuit cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out scones.
Place the scones on the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each scone.
Bake for 18-20 minutes, or until the scones are golden brown.
While the scones are baking, prepare the clotted cream mixture by whisking together clotted cream and a pinch of salt.
Once the scones are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool on a wire rack for a few minutes.
Split the scones in half horizontally and top with a dollop of clotted cream mixture and a few pieces of speck.
